purpose: Prove that the TraceGuard family exposes native oracles which can block unsupported, shallow, causally overclaimed, scope-overclaimed, or prediction-overclaimed temporal storylines when a task model explicitly selects them.

external_universe.membership_rule: Every failure class declared below is required, and every class owns exactly one target-native known-bad proof.

failure_classes.prevents: An event or trace with no usable supporting evidence is accepted as a supported storyline.
Caller-authored final status, support, rank, or causal license is accepted as inference input.
A syndicated or copied source from the same independence group accumulates as independent support.
Strong soft evidence compensates for an invalid-source or other hard admissibility constraint.
A backend failure silently switches to heuristic or legacy scoring and still emits a validated result.
A projection cites a factor or constraint that is absent from its canonical inference receipt.
A scenario perturbation is treated as effective without a distinct transformed problem and canonical inference receipt.
A time-order contradiction is hidden while the baseline storyline is licensed.
Chronological order is promoted to a causal conclusion without a mechanism and confounder review.
Broad closure passes while a model-derived critical perturbation remains unexecuted.
A broad trace passes with too few or badly distributed qualified event children.
An important causal storyline closes while a linked confounder remains unresolved.
A same-engine perturbation is accepted when its observed support delta contradicts its declared expected sensitivity.
Internal storyline perturbation is promoted to factual future prediction without a target-owned future holdout.
A bounded evaluation is presented as a broad storyline license.

universe_policies.inventory_rule: Project each canonical problem, solver-quality, solution, contribution, explanation, and perturbation receipt identity; every public projection must remain attached.
Project every TraceGuard native object-depth row; every critical object must be validated.
Project every model-derived perturbation candidate; every critical perturbation must be executed and validated.
Project every eligible event of every trace and enforce the target-native square-root event floor across early, middle, and late strata.

claim_boundary: A passing proof shows only that the current bundled TraceGuard family runtime accepts the family known-good and blocks the catalogued family regression cases. It does not choose what any task model must prevent, does not substitute for a task-model-instance purpose contract and proof, and does not establish factual truth for an arbitrary investigation.
